chief_complaint: A 35-year-old Asian male presents with recurrent episodes of acute pancreatitis.
history: The patient has a history of recurrent acute pancreatitis over the past 2 years. Endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) was performed, which revealed an anomalous biliopancreatic junction. The patient has no significant past medical history and is not on any medications. He is a non-smoker and does not consume excessive alcohol.
exam: Vital signs are within normal limits. Physical examination reveals no abnormalities.
labs: ERCP: Anomalous biliopancreatic junction. No other investigations were performed.
